Q Text: Give the SQL query that returns the title, duration and popularity of each Track of which its popularity is a at least 10 and at most 20.

Q Text: Give the SQL query that returns the year of the Album titled 'American Pie'
Answer: SELECT year FROM Album WHERE album_title = 'American Pie';

Q Text: Albums in Spotify are available for different markets. Give the SQL query that returns the country for each Market in which the Album 'American Pie' is available.
Answer: SELECT country FROM Album NATURAL JOIN Market WHERE album_title = 'American Pie';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that returns the title of each Track of which the Artist that Writes (wrote) the Track had the role 'lyricist' (So return tracks with lyricists).
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT Track.track_title FROM Track NATURAL JOIN Writes WHERE role = 'lyricist';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the title of each Album that is NOT available in the country 'DE'.
Answer: SELECT album_title FROM Album WHERE NOT 'DE' IN (SELECT country FROM Market WHERE Album.album_id = Market.album_id);

Q Text: Give the SQL query that return for each year the number of albums in that year.
Answer: SELECT year, COUNT(*) FROM Album GROUP BY year;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists for each Writer's role, the total number of times that this role occurs in the database. Do not list the number of time that the role is unknown.
Answer: SELECT role, count(*) FROM Writes WHERE role IS NOT NULL GROUP BY role;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT Artist.name, SUM(duration), AVG(popularity) FROM Artist NATURAL JOIN Performs NATURAL JOIN Track GROUP BY Artist.name ORDER BY SUM(duration) DESC LIMIT 5;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A , Performs Pe, Track T, Produces Pr, Writes W WHERE A.artist_id = Pe.artist_id AND A.artist_id = Pr.artist_id AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A , Performs Pe, Track T, Produces Pr, Writes W WHERE A.artist_id = Pe.artist_id AND Pe.track_id = T.track_id AND A.artist_id = Pr.artist_id AND Pr.track_id = T.track_id AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id AND W.track_id = T.track_id;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT artist.name,         SUM(track.duration) as total_duration,         AVG(track.popularity) as average_popularity FROM artist JOIN performs on artist.artist_id = performs.artist_id join track on performs.track_id = track.track_id GROUP BY artist.name ORDER BY total_duration DESC LIMIT 5;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the title of each Album that is NOT available in the country 'DE'.

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT Artist.name, SUM(Tracks.duration) as total_duration, AVG(Tracks.popularity) as avg_popularity FROM Artists JOIN Performs ON Artists.artist_id = Performs.artist_id JOIN Tracks ON Performs.track_id = Tracks.track_id GROUP BY Artist.name ORDER BY Total_duration DESC LIMIT 5;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A JOIN Performs P ON A.artist_id = P.artist_id JOIN Produces Pr ON A.artist_id = Pr.artist_id JOIN Writes W ON A.artist_id = W.artist_id;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A JOIN Performs P ON A.artist_id = P.artist_id JOIN Produces PR ON A.artist_id = PR.artist_id JOIN Writes W ON A.artist_id = W.artist_id JOIN Track T ON P.track_id = T.track_id AND PR.track_id = T.track_id AND W.track_id = T.track_id;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A JOIN Performs P ON A.artist_id = P.artist_id JOIN Produces PR ON A.artist_id = PR.artist_id JOIN Writes W ON A.artist_id = W.artist_id JOIN Track T ON P.track_id = T.track_id AND PR.track_id = T.track_id AND W.track_id = T.track_id;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that returns the title of each Track of which the Artist that Writes (wrote) the Track had the role 'lyricist' (So return tracks with lyricists).
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT track_title FROM Track, Writes WHERE Track.track_id = Writes.track_id AND role = 'lyricist';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the title of each Album that is NOT available in the country 'DE'.
Answer: SELECT album_title FROM Album WHERE NOT 'DE' IN (   SELECT country from Market   WHERE Album.album_id = Market.album_id );

Q Text: Give the SQL query that return for each year the number of albums in that year.
Answer: SELECT year, COUNT(*) FROM Album GROUP BY year;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists for each Writer's role, the total number of times that this role occurs in the database. Do not list the number of time that the role is unknown.
Answer: SELECT role, COUNT(*) FROM Writes WHERE NOT role IS NULL GROUP BY role;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT name, SUM(duration), AVG(popularity) FROM Artist, Performs, Track WHERE Artist.artist_id = Performs.artist_id AND Performs.track_id = Track.track_id GROUP BY name ORDER BY SUM(duration) DESC LIMIT 5;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A, Performs Pe, Produces Pr, Writes W WHERE A.artist_id = Pe.artist_id   AND A.artist_id = Pr.artist_id   AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A, Performs Pe, Produces Pr, Writes W, Track T WHERE A.artist_id = Pe.artist_id   AND A.artist_id = Pr.artist_id   AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id   AND Pe.track_id = T.track_id   AND Pr.track_id = T.Track_id   AND W.track_id = T.Track_id;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.* FROM Artist A JOIN Performs P ON A.artist_id = P.artist_id JOIN Produces R ON A.artist_id = R.artist_id JOIN Writes W ON A.artist_id = W.artist_id JOIN Track T ON P.track_id = T.track_id OR R.track_id = T.track_id OR W.track_id = T.track_id WHERE P.track_id IS NOT NULL AND R.track_id IS NOT NULL AND W.track_id IS NOT NULL;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.* FROM Artist A,Performs P,Produces R,Writes W,Track T WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id AND P.track_id=T.track_id AND A.artist_id = R.artist_id AND R.track_id=T.track_id AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id AND W.track_id=T.track_id;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT Artist.artist_id,Artist.name  FROM Artist,Writes,Produces,Performs  WHERE Artist.artist_id = Writes.artist_id  AND Artist.artist_id = Produces.artist_id  AND Artist.artist_id = Performs.artist_id;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.

Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.name From artist A, writes W, produces Pr, performs P WHERE  A.artist_id = W.artist_id AND A.artist_id = Pr.artist_id AND A.artist_id = P.artist_id AND P.track_id = Pr.track_id AND W.track_id = P.track_id;

Q Text: Albums in Spotify are available for different markets. Give the SQL query that returns the country for each Market in which the Album 'American Pie' is available.
Answer: SELECT M.country FROM Market M, Album A WHERE M.album_id = A.album_id AND A.album_title = 'American Pie';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that returns the title of each Track of which the Artist that Writes (wrote) the Track had the role 'lyricist' (So return tracks with lyricists).
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT T.track_title FROM Track T, Writes W WHERE T.track_id = W.track_id AND role = 'lyricist';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the title of each Album that is NOT available in the country 'DE'.
Answer: SELECT album_title FROM  Album WHERE  NOT 'DE' IN (SELECT country FROM Market WHERE Album.album_id = Market.album_id);

Q Text: Give the SQL query that return for each year the number of albums in that year.
Answer: SELECT COUNT (*), year FROM Album GROUP BY year ORDER BY year DESC;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists for each Writer's role, the total number of times that this role occurs in the database. Do not list the number of time that the role is unknown.
Answer: SELECT COUNT(*), role  FROM Writes WHERE  role IS NOT NULL GROUP BY role ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT A.name, SUM(T.duration) AS sum_duration, AVG(popularity) FROM Artist A JOIN Produces P ON A.artist_id = P.artist_id JOIN Track T ON P.track_id = T.track_id GROUP BY A.name ORDER BY sum_duration DESC LIMIT 5;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A, Performs P, Track T WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id AND P.track_id=T.track_id AND EXISTS (   SELECT *   FROM Produces R, Track T   WHERE A.artist_id = R.artist_id   AND R.track_id=T.track_id ) AND EXISTS (   SELECT *   FROM Writes W, Track T   WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id   AND W.track_id=T.track_id );

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A, Performs P, Track T, Produces R, Writes W WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id AND P.track_id=T.track_id AND A.artist_id = R.artist_id AND R.track_id=T.track_id AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id AND W.track_id=T.track_id;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that returns the title of each Track of which the Artist that Writes (wrote) the Track had the role 'lyricist' (So return tracks with lyricists).
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT track_title FROM Track INNER JOIN Writes ON Writes.track_id = Track.track_id WHERE Role = 'lyricist';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the title of each Album that is NOT available in the country 'DE'.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT Album.album_title FROM Album WHERE NOT EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM market WHERE Album.album_id = market.album_id AND market.Country = 'DE');

Q Text: Give the SQL query that return for each year the number of albums in that year.

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists for each Writer's role, the total number of times that this role occurs in the database. Do not list the number of time that the role is unknown.

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT artist.name AS artist_name, SUM(Track.duration) AS seconds, AVG(Track.popularity) AS popularity FROM artist JOIN Performs ON Artist.artist_id = Performs.artist_id JOIN Track ON Performs.track_id = Track.track_id GROUP BY artist_name ORDER BY seconds DESC LIMIT 5;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A JOIN Performs P ON A.artist_id = P.artist_id JOIN Track T1 ON P.track_id = T1.track_id JOIN Produces Pr ON A.artist_id = Pr.artist_id JOIN Track T2 ON Pr.track_id = T2.track_id JOIN Writes W ON A.artist_id = W.artist_id JOIN Track T3 ON W.track_id = T3.track_id;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT Artist.name FROM Artist  JOIN Performs ON Artist.artist_id = Performs.artist_id JOIN Produces ON Artist.artist_id = Produces.artist_id JOIN Writes ON Artist.artist_id = Writes.artist_id WHERE Performs.track_id = Produces.track_id  AND Performs.track_id = Writes.track_id;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT Artist.name, SUM(Track.duration), AVG(popularity)  FROM Artist, Track, Performs  WHERE Artist.artist_id = Performs.artist_id  AND Track.track_id = Performs.track_id  GROUP BY Artist.name ORDER BY SUM(Track.duration) DESC  LIMIT 5;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A WHERE EXISTS (   SELECT *   FROM Performs P, Produces Pr, Track T, Writes W   WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id   AND P.track_id=T.track_id   AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id   AND W.track_id=T.track_id   AND P.track_id=T.track_id   AND Pr.artist_id=A.artist_id );

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...

Q Text: Give the SQL query that returns the year of the Album titled 'American Pie'
Answer: SELECT year FROM Album WHERE album_title = 'American Pie';

Q Text: Albums in Spotify are available for different markets. Give the SQL query that returns the country for each Market in which the Album 'American Pie' is available.
Answer: SELECT country FROM Market,Album WHERE Album.album_title = 'American Pie' AND Album.album_id = Market.album_id;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that returns the title of each Track of which the Artist that Writes (wrote) the Track had the role 'lyricist' (So return tracks with lyricists).
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT track_title FROM Track,Writes WHERE Writes.role = 'lyricists' AND Writes.track_id = Track.track_id;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the title of each Album that is NOT available in the country 'DE'.
Answer: SELECT album_title FROM Album,Market WHERE Album.album_id = Market.album_id AND NOT Market.country = 'DE';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that return for each year the number of albums in that year.
Answer: SELECT year, COUNT(*) FROM Album WHERE NOT Album.year IS NULL GROUP BY Album.year;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists for each Writer's role, the total number of times that this role occurs in the database. Do not list the number of time that the role is unknown.
Answer: SELECT role, COUNT(*) FROM Writes WHERE NOT Writes.role IS NULL GROUP BY Writes.role;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT name, SUM(duration) total, AVG(popularity) FROM Artist, Track, Performs WHERE Artist.artist_id = Performs.artist_id AND Performs.track_id = Track.track_id GROUP BY Artist.name ORDER BY total DESC LIMIT 5;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT name FROM Artist A, Performs Pe, Produces Pr, Writes W WHERE A.artist_id = Pe.artist_id AND A.artist_id = Pr.artist_id AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT name FROM Artist A, Performs Pe, Produces Pr, Writes W WHERE A.artist_id = Pe.artist_id AND A.artist_id = Pr.artist_id AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id AND Pe.track_id = Pr.track_id AND Pe.track_id = W.track_id;

Answer: SELECT DISTINCT Artist.name FROM Artist, Performs, Produces, Writes WHERE Artist.artist_id = Performs.artist_id AND Artist.artist_id = Produces.artist_id AND Artist.artist_id = Writes.artist_id;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT Artist.name FROM Artist, Performs, Produces, Writes WHERE Artist.artist_id = Performs.artist_id AND Artist.artist_id = Produces.artist_id AND Artist.artist_id = Writes.artist_id AND Performs.track_id = Produces.track_id;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT Artist.name, sum(duration), AVG(Track.popularity) FROM Artist, Track, Performs WHERE Performs.track_id = Track.track_id AND Performs.artist_id = Artist.artist_id GROUP BY Artist.name ORDER BY sum(duration) DESC LIMIT 5;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A JOIN Performs P ON A.artist_id = P.artist_id JOIN Track T1 ON P.track_id = T1.track_id JOIN Produces Pr ON A.artist_id = Pr.artist_id JOIN Track T2 ON Pr.track_id = T2.track_id JOIN Writes W ON A.artist_id = W.artist_id JOIN Track T3 ON W.track_id = T3.track_id;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Performs P, Artist A,Produces Pf,Writes W WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id AND A.artist_id = Pf.artist_id AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Performs P, Artist A,Produces Pf,Writes W WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id AND A.artist_id = Pf.artist_id AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id AND P.track_id = Pf.track_id AND P.track_id = W.track_id;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that returns the title, duration and popularity of each Track of which its popularity is a at least 10 and at most 20.

Q Text: Albums in Spotify are available for different markets. Give the SQL query that returns the country for each Market in which the Album 'American Pie' is available.
Answer: SELECT m.country FROM Markets m JOIN AlbumMarketAvailability ama ON m.market_id = ama.market_id JOIN Albums a ON ama.album_id = a.album_id WHERE a.title = 'American Pie';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that returns the title of each Track of which the Artist that Writes (wrote) the Track had the role 'lyricist' (So return tracks with lyricists).
Answer: SELECT t.title FROM Tracks t JOIN TrackArtists ta ON t.track_id = ta.track_id JOIN Artists a ON ta.artist_id = a.artist_id WHERE ta.role = 'lyricist';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the title of each Album that is NOT available in the country 'DE'.

Q Text: Give the SQL query that return for each year the number of albums in that year.

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists for each Writer's role, the total number of times that this role occurs in the database. Do not list the number of time that the role is unknown.

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT     a.artist_name AS ArtistName,     SUM(t.duration) AS TotalDuration,     AVG(t.popularity) AS AveragePopularity FROM     Artists a JOIN     TrackArtists ta ON a.artist_id = ta.artist_id JOIN     Tracks t ON ta.track_id = t.track_id GROUP BY     a.artist_id, a.artist_name ORDER BY     TotalDuration DESC LIMIT 5;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the title of each Album that is NOT available in the country 'DE'.
Answer: SELECT album_title FROM Album WHERE NOT 'DE' IN (     SELECT country FROM Market     WHERE Market.album_id == Album.album_id);

Q Text: Give the SQL query that return for each year the number of albums in that year.

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists for each Writer's role, the total number of times that this role occurs in the database. Do not list the number of time that the role is unknown.
Answer: SELECT role, count(*) FROM Writes WHERE NOT role IS NULL GROUP BY role;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT name, SUM(duration) as total_duration, AVG(popularity) as average_popularity FROM Artist, Track, Performs WHERE Artist.artist_id == Performs.artist_id AND Track.track_id == Performs.track_id GROUP BY name ORDER BY total_duration DESC LIMIT 5;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A, Performs Pe, Produces Pr, Writes W WHERE A.artist_id = Pe.artist_id AND A.artist_id = Pr.artist_id AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A, Performs Pe, Produces Pr, Writes W, Track T WHERE A.artist_id = Pe.artist_id AND A.artist_id = Pr.artist_id AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id AND Pe.track_id=T.track_id AND Pr.track_id=T.track_id AND W.track_id=T.track_id;

Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A, Performs Per, Track T, Produces Pro, Writes W WHERE EXISTS (A.artist_id = Per.artist_id AND Per.track_id = T.track_id) AND EXISTS (A.artist_id = Pro.artist_id AND Pro.track_id = T.track_id) AND EXISTS (A.artist_id = W.artist_id AND W.track_id = T.track_id)

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A, Performs Per, Track T, Produces Pro, Writes W WHERE A.artist_id = Per.artist_id AND Per.track_id = T.track_id AND A.artist_id = Pro.artist_id AND Pro.track_id = T.track_id AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id AND W.track_id = T.track_id

Q Text: Give the SQL query that returns the title, duration and popularity of each Track of which its popularity is a at least 10 and at most 20.
Answer: SELECT Track.track_title, Track.duration, Track.popularity FROM Track Where Track.popularity BETWEEN 10 AND 20;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that returns the year of the Album titled 'American Pie'
Answer: SELECT Album.year FROM Album Where Album.album_title = 'American Pie';

Q Text: Albums in Spotify are available for different markets. Give the SQL query that returns the country for each Market in which the Album 'American Pie' is available.
Answer: SELECT Market.country FROM Market, Album WHERE Market.album_id = Album.album_id AND Album.album_title = 'American Pie';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that returns the title of each Track of which the Artist that Writes (wrote) the Track had the role 'lyricist' (So return tracks with lyricists).
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT Track.track_title FROM Track, Writes WHERE Track.track_id = Writes.track_id AND Writes.role = 'lyricist';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the title of each Album that is NOT available in the country 'DE'.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT Album.album_title FROM Album WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT *                   FROM Market                   WHERE Album.album_id = Market.album_id                   AND Market.country = 'DE'                  );

Q Text: Give the SQL query that return for each year the number of albums in that year.
Answer: SELECT Album.year, COUNT(*) FROM Album GROUP BY Album.year;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists for each Writer's role, the total number of times that this role occurs in the database. Do not list the number of time that the role is unknown.
Answer: SELECT Writes.role, COUNT(*) FROM Writes WHERE Writes.role IS NOT NULL GROUP BY Writes.role;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT Artist.name, SUM(Track.duration) AS total_duration, AVG(Track.popularity) AS average_popularity FROM Artist, Performs, Track  WHERE Artist.artist_id = Performs.artist_id and Performs.track_id = Track.track_id GROUP BY Artist.name ORDER BY total_duration DESC LIMIT 5;
